subject: Public Health
title: Household Water Storage
description: A short lesson to help you apply Household Water Storage.

hook.text: Water can be clean at the source but contaminated by the time it reaches your cup at home.

buildup.text: Open containers, dirty hands, and insects introduce pathogens during transport and storage.

discovery.text: Narrow-necked containers with taps prevent hand contact—reducing recontamination by up to 50%.

twist.text: Solar disinfection—filling clear bottles and leaving them in sunlight—kills most pathogens in 6 hours.

climax.text: Safe storage is the last mile of water safety—without it, treatment at the source means nothing.

punchline.text: Clean water at the source means nothing if storage recontaminates it.

quiz.question: How do narrow-necked containers help water safety?
options: They prevent hand contact, reducing recontamination by up to 50%
